independence: high (deterministic Crossref existence check; no model judgment)
finding: PRECISE but coverage-bounded. 0.0% clean over-block and HIGH independence: it abstains ONLY when an answer cites a study it cannot confirm exists. Overall catch 9.3% because (a) the strong answer model often DEBUNKS rather than repeating the fabricated citation (no citation in the output to catch — arguably correct, the output is clean), and (b) when a citation IS repeated, the verifier catches SPECIFIC fabricated studies (named author/institution+year no real work matches, e.g. 'the 2019 Kessler study') but MISSES vague ones ('a recent study') and fabrications about well-studied topics where real topical papers cause a false confirmation. It is the right HIGH-INDEPENDENCE tool for the authority-laundering style, contributing catch with zero over-block — NOT a silver bullet.
trustworthy_principle: Never vouch for a citation the system cannot independently confirm (fail-closed).
